Article archive

Urban Gardening article archive

This is the plain utility list for every published urban gardening guide. For the main topic page with curated pathways, featured entry points, and broader context, use the primary hub.

Primary topic hub

Urban Gardening

Urban gardening is not one technique. It is a set of design decisions around light, root volume, watering, and crop choice inside constrained spaces. This hub organizes the topic into beginner guidance, setup decisions, troubleshooting, and more advanced optimization.

Open topic hub Jump to the archive list
13 articles

All published urban gardening guides

Browse the full set of published guides in Urban Gardening. This archive stays intentionally simple so it does not compete with the main hub.

Urban Gardening March 25, 2026

North-Facing Window Herbs: What Actually Works and When You Need a Grow Light

A north-facing window is widely considered the death sentence of an indoor garden. But with the right selection of shade-tolerant herbs and strategic placement, you can still harvest fresh greens year-round.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Urban Gardening March 21, 2026

When to Prune Basil, Mint, Parsley, and Chives for Better Growth

Pruning is the most misunderstood part of indoor gardening. If you pick individual basil leaves, the plant grows tall and leggy. If you cut the main stem correctly, it explodes into a dense, bushy harvest.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Urban Gardening March 20, 2026

How Often Should You Water Potted Herbs? (Stop Guessing)

The most common way beginners kill potted herbs is by watering them on a strict calendar schedule. Throw away the calendar and learn the 'weight test' instead.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Urban Gardening March 16, 2026

4 Best Herbs for a Sunny Balcony That Survive High Heat

A sunny balcony is perfect for herbs, but the intense heat and wind require the right crop choices. Start with basil, thyme, and rosemary for reliable harvests.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide
Urban Gardening March 15, 2026

Best Herbs for Low-Light Kitchens and Windowsills

Stop trying to force basil to grow in the dark. If your kitchen gets limited indirect light, stick to these forgiving herbs like parsley, chives, and mint.

Urban Harvest Lab Editorial Read guide

A practical windowsill herb guide for homes that feel bright enough to people but still struggle to keep basil and other herbs compact without extra help.

A practical apartment herb guide for people growing entirely indoors, with realistic advice on light, layout, containers, and everyday maintenance.
